scenario_results.notes: Draft structure suggestions include objects, links, facts, and events with source evidence and confidence, without approved ontology truth.
Promotion creates durable draft ontology items preserving evidence, owner scope, namespace, and trust state.
Operational map includes evidence-linked artifacts, ontology items, claims, missions, actions, timelines, policies, decisions, and workflows.
Contradictory policy evidence is visible with competing values and evidence, unresolved status, and no silent winner.
A newer evidence bundle marks the approved claim as needing review and prevents current-truth use without warning.
Ontology item changes record version, diff, impact, affected objects, rollback guidance, migration guidance, and audit.
Unknown-domain extraction stays draft, labels unsupported inferences, and records evidence gaps instead of claiming domain certainty.

nodes.label: Atlas Renewal governed by review window
Review the Atlas Renewal window with evidence-linked operational context.

may_do: run allowed low-risk internal actions in Autopilot mode

escalation_rules: Escalate high-risk, destructive, sensitive, cross-namespace, or external writeback actions.
Escalate when evidence coverage is insufficient or policy decision is deny.
evidence_expectations: Evidence Bundle with at least one artifact reference.
Dry-run diff and expected impact before side-effecting action.
Audit refs for every policy and action transition.
mission.goal: Review the Atlas Renewal window with evidence-linked operational context.
mission.review_cadence: owner review required before high-risk execution; after-action audit after every run
stop_conditions: Policy denies scope, egress, connector capability, data sensitivity, or workspace mode.
A requested action is outside the mission contract.
success_criteria: Use attached evidence refs for every durable claim or action.
Record every action proposal, dry-run, policy decision, approval, result, and audit event.
